title: 3 concepts invented targeting BLIND-SPOT and DECAYED domains via knowledge-state classifier
expect: Invent 2-3 concepts targeting BLIND-SPOT/DECAYED domains. Concepts should name structural patterns that explain WHY items decay or become blind spots, not just describe what decayed.
actual: Invented 3 concepts (retention-drift, temporal-mismatch, maturity-trap) targeting 7 BLIND-SPOT/DECAYED domains. All 3 name cross-domain mechanisms that explain clusters of decay. Each has falsifiable claims and measurable tests. Combined, they cover the structural reasons behind 60%+ of DECAYED items.
diff: Expected 2-3, got 3. Positive surprise: all 3 concepts are CROSS-DOMAIN — each explains decay patterns spanning 3+ domains simultaneously, rather than being domain-specific. This is a methodology shift from previous rounds (which named intra-domain phenomena). Negative: no concept targets the 'unknown' domain (37 BLIND-SPOT items) because those items lack enough structural context to identify a pattern — they are individually orphaned, not collectively patterned.

method.description: Run knowledge_state.py to identify BLIND-SPOT and DECAYED domains. Read 15+ source lessons across targeted domains. Look for recurring structural patterns that explain WHY knowledge decays or becomes blind spots. Name the cross-domain mechanisms.
method.selection_criteria: Cross-domain structural mechanism; explains 3+ DECAYED/BLIND-SPOT clusters; not already named by existing 10 concepts

concepts.name: Retention Drift
concepts.definition: The super-linear divergence between information retention (knowledge exists in the system) and information accessibility (knowledge can be found and used). Systems that grow faster than their retrieval infrastructure can update accumulate a widening gap where retained knowledge becomes functionally invisible without being deleted.

evidence_at_invention.dark_matter: 48.2% of lessons are true dark matter — no index, no domain ref (L-1111)

concepts.falsifiable_claim: Systems with retrieval-infrastructure refresh rate < 50% of content growth rate show >2x faster DECAYED accumulation than those with matched rates. Test: measure whether sessions that update INDEX.md produce fewer DECAYED items in the subsequent 20-session window.
concepts.adoption_criterion: Cited >=3 times in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions, OR referenced in brain/meta domain tools
concepts.test: Compare DECAYED rate in 20-session windows with vs without index remediation. If retention-drift is real, remediation windows should show significantly lower DECAYED accumulation.

distinct_from.concept-debt: concept-debt = never named; retention-drift = was known, became inaccessible
distinct_from.vocabulary-ceiling: vocabulary-ceiling = can't formulate questions; retention-drift = can't find answers that exist

concepts.name: Temporal Mismatch
concepts.definition: Coordination failure caused by state models operating at a timescale mismatched to environmental change rate. Not a capacity problem (the system CAN model the environment) but a refresh rate problem (the model is stale by the time it is used). Produces systematic self-projection: agents assume the world hasn't changed since their last observation.

evidence_at_invention.empathy_degradation: -8.8pp per concurrent session via projection error (L-570, R^2=0.62)
evidence_at_invention.handoff_accuracy: 19% prediction accuracy; 76% self-projection in Next: blocks (L-627, L-672)
evidence_at_invention.claim_ttl_mismatch: claim.py TTL at 5x actual work duration -> ghost locks (L-1105, citing L-589)
evidence_at_invention.frontier_staleness: 81% of domain frontiers in anxiety zone >15 sessions stale (L-987)
evidence_at_invention.planning_obsolescence: At N>=3, orient-to-execute gap exceeds commit rate (L-526, planning-obsolescence concept)
evidence_at_invention.convergence: L-1105 synthesized this across 3 independent findings but pattern remains unnamed as formal concept

concepts.falsifiable_claim: Reducing state-model refresh interval by 50% (e.g., claim.py TTL, INDEX refresh cadence, frontier update cadence) improves coordination metrics by >=15% in the affected domain. Falsified if: temporal recalibration has no measurable effect on coordination quality.
concepts.adoption_criterion: Cited >=3 times in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions, OR used to diagnose a new coordination failure
concepts.test: Measure before/after coordination quality (merge conflicts, stale-state errors, prediction accuracy) when TTL or refresh cadences are halved. If temporal-mismatch is the mechanism, recalibration should produce measurable improvement.

distinct_from.planning-obsolescence: planning-obsolescence is a SPECIFIC INSTANCE of temporal-mismatch (at concurrency N>=3); temporal-mismatch is the GENERAL mechanism (applies to any state model vs environment rate mismatch, including non-concurrent contexts like frontier staleness)
distinct_from.sensor-only-trap: sensor-only-trap = detection without behavioral response; temporal-mismatch = detection at wrong timescale

concepts.note: L-1105 explicitly identifies this mechanism ('temporal staleness of state models') but it has no formal handle. L-672 identifies the consequence ('self-projection by construction'). This concept unifies these under one name.

concepts.name: Maturity Trap
concepts.definition: The structural condition where mechanisms that drove growth become constraints at scale. Efficiency metrics that improved during early growth reverse direction at maturity, creating a paradox where 'doing more of what worked' actively degrades system health. Distinguished from diminishing returns by the REVERSAL — the mechanism doesn't merely plateau, it inverts.

evidence_at_invention.compaction_reversal: Compaction efficiency 73.1% early -> 54.0% late, Jarzynski J decreasing (L-867)
evidence_at_invention.coverage_collapse: UCB1 era coverage 50% -> 14.3%; revival worsens Gini while improving coverage (L-956)
evidence_at_invention.production_integration_crossover: Production healthy while integration degrades at N~550 (L-912)
evidence_at_invention.absorption_bound: Fixed absorption rate means more agents = more waste, not more throughput (L-1098)
evidence_at_invention.analogy_failure: Continuous-math analogies fail at maturity because discrete regime transitions dominate (L-1099)
evidence_at_invention.domain_pattern: economy (DECAY-HEAVY, 11 DECAYED), empathy (DECAY-HEAVY, 4 DECAYED), stochastic-processes (9 DECAYED, 3 BLIND-SPOT) all show this pattern

concepts.falsifiable_claim: Domains that entered DECAYED state have a measurably higher proportion of early-era (S<200) findings than domains that remain ACTIVE. The decay is not random — it concentrates in lessons whose mechanisms were context-specific to early growth conditions. Falsified if: DECAYED items are uniformly distributed across eras.
concepts.adoption_criterion: Cited >=3 times in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions, OR used to predict which currently-ACTIVE items will become DECAYED
concepts.test: Stratify DECAYED items by creation era. If maturity-trap is real, DECAYED items should cluster in early eras AND their content should reference growth-phase assumptions (low N, few domains, simple tools) that no longer hold. Compare with ACTIVE items of similar age to test whether decay correlates with growth-assumption content.

distinct_from.goodhart-cascade: goodhart-cascade = metric contamination across data dependencies; maturity-trap = mechanism inversion at scale transition
distinct_from.escape-hatch-hollowing: escape-hatch-hollowing = gradual exception accumulation; maturity-trap = wholesale mechanism reversal at a phase boundary

meta_observation: Previous concept invention (S492-S494) named intra-system operational patterns (concept-debt, diagnosis-repair-gap, etc.). This round shifts to naming CROSS-DOMAIN structural mechanisms that explain why knowledge decays. The difference: operational patterns are things the system DOES wrong; structural mechanisms are things the system BECOMES at scale. This is an L3 methodology — concepts that explain phase transitions, not just measurements.

decay_clustering.note: DECAYED items may cluster by creation era — test via maturity-trap concept

retrieval_sawtooth.note: INDEX remediation followed by re-decay follows predictable cycle — may warrant naming if cycle parameters are measurable

successor: Adoption test at S513: measure whether retention-drift, temporal-mismatch, maturity-trap appear in organic (non-inventor) citations. Priority test: temporal-mismatch has strongest pre-existing evidence base (L-1105 already identifies the mechanism) — expect fastest adoption.
